title: Aureline semantic-readiness view and why-not-ready inspector contract
description: Boundary schema for the semantic-readiness inspector view. The frozen semantic-readiness vocabulary (semantic_readiness_state, not_ready_reason, safe_next_action) and its canonical carrier record live in schemas/filesystem/save_target_token.schema.json and docs/filesystem/filesystem_identity_vocabulary.md; this schema does not redefine those tokens. Instead it defines the readiness-inspector view record that wraps one canonical semantic_readiness_record and adds a typed why_not_ready block: blocking_subsystem, freshness_source, confidence_class, safe_next_actions, and an escalation_target (docs linkback or export route). The intent is cross-surface parity: shell, search, diagnostics, migration, and support/export surfaces consume the same parity-floor fields without compressing partial/stale/imported/heuristic/policy-blocked/out-of-scope states into a generic unavailable label. Adding a new blocking_subsystem, freshness_source, confidence_class token, or escalation_target_kind is additive-minor and bumps semantic_readiness_view_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

semantic_readiness_view_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the semantic-readiness view vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque equality-comparable string.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Never carries raw paths or raw provider URLs.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional human-review metadata block carried by fixtures.

confidence_class.description: Coarse confidence class aligned with the confidence vocabulary used by graph-backed surfaces. A non-exact readiness state typically caps confidence; consumers MUST NOT silently upgrade.

blocking_subsystem.description: Typed subsystem lane currently gating readiness. Used by inspector grouping, diagnostics routing, and support escalation.

freshness_source.description: Typed signal that asserted staleness/partiality/unavailability. This is a disclosure aid; it does not replace semantic_readiness.state or not_ready_reason.

escalation_target_kind.description: Typed escalation / linkback destination class. docs_reference points to a docs path; command_id points to a command plane id; support_bundle_export points to an export route; none is admissible only when safe_next_actions contain no_action_required.

ref.description: Opaque reference. For docs_reference it is a workspace-relative docs path; for command_id it is a command plane id; for support_bundle_export it is an export route ref; null only when kind==none.

label.description: Optional short label rendered alongside the escalation target.

escalation_target.description: Typed escalation / docs linkback destination. Consumers render this as a stable link rather than synthesizing free-form URLs.

safe_next_action_row_ref.description: Projection slot reused from the filesystem identity vocabulary: one safe-next-action row with action, command_id, and optional consequence.

semantic_readiness_record_ref.description: Canonical semantic readiness record (subject + semantic_readiness frame) that every surface consumes.

not_ready_explainer_copy_id.description: Stable copy id from artifacts/filesystem/readiness_explainer_copy_ids.yaml. Screenshot-safe UI, CLI, and export surfaces may use this id to retrieve localized copy while still preserving parity.

safe_next_actions.description: Ordered list of safe-next-action rows offered by the inspector. This list must not widen or contradict the canonical readiness record's safe_next_actions when both are present.

notes.description: Optional redaction-aware note rendered alongside the why-not-ready rows.

why_not_ready.description: Typed why-not-ready block for the readiness inspector. Complements the canonical readiness vocabulary without redefining it.

view_id.description: Stable view identifier for the inspector instance. Opaque id.

why_not_ready.description: Typed why-not-ready block. Required whenever readiness_record.semantic_readiness.state != exact.

support_ref.description: Optional opaque reference to a support bundle entry or exported packet that contains this record.

semantic_readiness_view_record.description: Semantic-readiness inspector view record. Wraps one canonical semantic_readiness_record and adds a typed why-not-ready block to make blocking subsystem, freshness source, confidence class, and escalation linkback explicit.
